methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: True Anomaly
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Hidden Level

data.narrative:
Fortem Technologies is a Series C-stage defense and security company that has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 11 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including True Anomaly, Anduril Industries, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $100M total raised proxy, implying a $170M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 11-company comp set yields a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B. The peer set includes Series B and Series D companies; relevant same-stage peers like True Anomaly (Series C, $1.5B) anchor the upper band.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $144M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $785M. The wide spread reflects material stage and performance heterogeneity within the defense-tech peer set. Key uncertainties include revenue scale relative to peers, customer concentration within government channels, and technology differentiation within a crowded frontier-defense sector. Stage-progression and peer-median methods diverge significantly, indicating sensitivity to the base assumption and comp selection.
